outputs.document.md:
SDTM LB Domain Specification
Document Version: 1.0
Date: 2025-04-10
Study: XYZ-123
Number of Records: 54,828

1. Domain Overview
The LB (Laboratory Test Results) domain contains 54,828 records representing all laboratory assessments collected during the study. This specification defines the mapping from source data to SDTM LB variables, including unit conversions, reference range handling, specimen types, and supplemental qualifier mappings.

2. LBTESTCD Mapping
LBTESTCD is a unique, short character code for each lab test. Mapping is based on standardized terminology (e.g., CDISC SDTM LB Test Code codelist). The following table defines the mapping for all tests in the domain:

| LBTESTCD | LBTEST | Original Source Test Name | Comments |
|----------|--------|--------------------------|----------|
| ALB | Albumin | Serum Albumin | Standard code |
| ALP | Alkaline Phosphatase | Alk Phos | Standard code |
| ALT | Alanine Aminotransferase | ALT (SGPT) | Standard code |
| AST | Aspartate Aminotransferase | AST (SGOT) | Standard code |
| BILI | Bilirubin | Total Bilirubin | Standard code |
| BUN | Blood Urea Nitrogen | BUN | Standard code |
| CA | Calcium | Calcium, Total | Standard code |
| CHOL | Cholesterol | Total Cholesterol | Standard code |
| CL | Chloride | Chloride | Standard code |
| CREAT | Creatinine | Creatinine | Standard code |
| GLUC | Glucose | Glucose | Standard code |
| HCT | Hematocrit | Hematocrit | Standard code |
| HGB | Hemoglobin | Hemoglobin | Standard code |
| K | Potassium | Potassium | Standard code |
| NA | Sodium | Sodium | Standard code |
| PHOS | Phosphorus | Phosphorus | Standard code |
| PLAT | Platelet Count | Platelets | Standard code |
| RBC | Red Blood Cell Count | RBC | Standard code |
| TBIL | Bilirubin, Total | Total Bilirubin | Alternative code for BILI |
| TP | Total Protein | Total Protein | Standard code |
| UA | Uric Acid | Uric Acid | Standard code |
| WBC | White Blood Cell Count | WBC | Standard code |

Note: All LBTESTCD values are derived from the CDISC SDTM LB Test Code codelist (C66789). For tests not in the codelist, a sponsor-defined code is used and documented in the supplemental qualifier.

3. Unit Conversion Logic
All laboratory results are converted to standard SI units for SDTM submission. The conversion logic is as follows:

- If the original unit is already SI (e.g., mmol/L, g/L, 10^9/L), no conversion is applied.
- If the original unit is conventional (e.g., mg/dL, g/dL, %), conversion factors are applied per standard clinical chemistry and hematology guidelines.

| LBTESTCD | Original Unit | Standard Unit | Conversion Factor | Formula |
|----------|---------------|---------------|-------------------|---------|
| ALB | g/dL | g/L | 10 | Multiply by 10 |
| ALP | U/L | U/L | 1 | No conversion |
| ALT | U/L | U/L | 1 | No conversion |
| AST | U/L | U/L | 1 | No conversion |
| BILI | mg/dL | µmol/L | 17.1 | Multiply by 17.1 |
| BUN | mg/dL | mmol/L | 0.357 | Multiply by 0.357 |
| CA | mg/dL | mmol/L | 0.2495 | Multiply by 0.2495 |
| CHOL | mg/dL | mmol/L | 0.02586 | Multiply by 0.02586 |
| CL | mEq/L | mmol/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |
| CREAT | mg/dL | µmol/L | 88.4 | Multiply by 88.4 |
| GLUC | mg/dL | mmol/L | 0.05551 | Multiply by 0.05551 |
| HCT | % | 1 (fraction) | 0.01 | Multiply by 0.01 |
| HGB | g/dL | g/L | 10 | Multiply by 10 |
| K | mEq/L | mmol/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |
| NA | mEq/L | mmol/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |
| PHOS | mg/dL | mmol/L | 0.3229 | Multiply by 0.3229 |
| PLAT | 10^3/µL | 10^9/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |
| RBC | 10^6/µL | 10^12/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |
| TBIL | mg/dL | µmol/L | 17.1 | Multiply by 17.1 |
| TP | g/dL | g/L | 10 | Multiply by 10 |
| UA | mg/dL | µmol/L | 59.48 | Multiply by 59.48 |
| WBC | 10^3/µL | 10^9/L | 1 | No conversion (equivalent) |

Conversion is applied at the record level. The original unit and result are preserved in the supplemental qualifier (see Section 6). The converted result is stored in LBORRES (original result) with unit in LBORRESU, and the standardized result is stored in LBSTRESC (character) and LBSTRESN (numeric) with unit in LBSTRESU.

4. Reference Range Handling
Reference ranges are provided by the central laboratory and are specific to the specimen type, age, and sex of the subject. The following rules apply:

- LBORNRLO and LBORNRHI: Original reference range lower and upper limits (in original units). These are populated from the source data as provided by the lab.
- LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI: Standardized reference range lower and upper limits (in standard units). These are derived by applying the same conversion factor as the result to the original reference range limits.
- If the original reference range is not provided (e.g., for some qualitative tests), LBORNRLO, LBORNRHI, LBSTNRLO, and LBSTNRHI are left null.
- For tests where reference ranges are age- or sex-specific, the appropriate range is selected based on subject demographics at the time of the assessment. If multiple ranges apply, the most specific range is used.
- If the reference range is a single value (e.g., ">X"), only the lower or upper limit is populated accordingly.
- Reference range handling is documented in the metadata for each test.

5. Specimen Type
The specimen type is captured in LBSPEC. The following values are used, based on the CDISC SDTM LB Specimen Type codelist (C66790):

| LBSPEC | Description | Source Mapping |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| SERUM | Serum | All serum chemistry tests |
| PLASMA | Plasma | Plasma-based tests (e.g., glucose) |
| URINE | Urine | Urinalysis tests |
| WHOLE BLOOD | Whole Blood | Hematology tests (CBC) |
| CSF | Cerebrospinal Fluid | CSF-specific tests (if applicable) |
| OTHER | Other | Any other specimen type |

For this study, the majority of records (approximately 50,000) are from SERUM or WHOLE BLOOD. The remaining records are from URINE or PLASMA. Specimen type is derived from the source data based on the lab test panel.

6. Supplemental Qualifier Mappings
Supplemental qualifiers are used to capture additional data that do not fit into the standard SDTM LB variables. The following supplemental qualifiers are defined:

| QNAM | QLABEL | QVAL | Mapping Rule |
|------|--------|------|--------------|
| LBORRESU | Original Result Unit | Original unit of measurement | Populated from source data before conversion |
| LBORNRLO | Original Reference Range Lower Limit | Original lower limit | Populated from source data |
| LBORNRHI | Original Reference Range Upper Limit | Original upper limit | Populated from source data |
| LBMETHOD | Method of Test | Test method description | Populated from source data if available |
| LBCAT | Category of Test | e.g., CHEMISTRY, HEMATOLOGY, URINALYSIS | Derived from test panel |
| LBSCAT | Subcategory of Test | e.g., LIVER FUNCTION, RENAL FUNCTION | Derived from test panel |
| LBFAST | Fasting Status | Y/N/UNK | Populated from source data if available |
| LBLAT | Latency | Time since last dose (if applicable) | Populated for PK-related labs |
| LBREASND | Reason Not Done | Reason if test not performed | Populated if LBSTAT = "NOT DONE" |
| LBNAM | Vendor Name | Name of laboratory | Populated from source data |
| LBLOINC | LOINC Code | LOINC code for the test | Populated from source data if available |

Supplemental qualifiers are stored in the SUPP-- dataset (SUPPLB) with:
- RDOMAIN = "LB"
- IDVAR = "LBSEQ"
- IDVARVAL = LBSEQ value for the corresponding record
- QNAM, QLABEL, QVAL as defined above
- QORIG = "CRF" or "DERIVED" as appropriate
- QEVAL = null (unless evaluator is specified)

7. Additional Mapping Details
- LBSEQ: Unique sequence number per subject, assigned sequentially within each subject.
- LBTEST: Full name of the test, derived from LBTESTCD using the codelist.
- LBCAT: Category of test (e.g., CHEMISTRY, HEMATOLOGY, URINALYSIS).
- LBSCAT: Subcategory (e.g., LIVER FUNCTION, RENAL FUNCTION, CBC).
- LBMETHOD: Method of test (e.g., "COLORIMETRIC", "ION-SELECTIVE ELECTRODE").
- LBORRES: Original result as collected (character).
- LBORRESU: Original unit (character).
- LBSTRESC: Standardized result (character).
- LBSTRESN: Standardized result (numeric).
- LBSTRESU: Standardized unit (character).
- LBSTNRLO: Standardized reference range lower limit (numeric).
- LBSTNRHI: Standardized reference range upper limit (numeric).
- LBSTNRC: Reference range comment (if applicable).
- LBNRIND: Reference range indicator (LOW, NORMAL, HIGH, etc.), derived by comparing LBSTRESN to LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI.
- LBSTAT: Completion status (e.g., "NOT DONE" if test not performed).
- LBREASND: Reason not done (if LBSTAT = "NOT DONE").
- LBLOBXFL: Last observation before exposure flag (populated per study rules).
- LBBLFL: Baseline flag (populated per study rules).
- LBDRVFL: Derived flag (if result is derived, e.g., calculated).
- LBTOX: Toxicity grade (if applicable, per CTCAE).
- LBTOXGR: Toxicity grade description.

8. Data Quality and Validation
- All LBTESTCD values are validated against the CDISC SDTM LB Test Code codelist.
- Unit conversions are validated by double programming.
- Reference range handling is reviewed by a clinical scientist.
- Supplemental qualifiers are documented in the define.xml.

End of Specification.
